per_turn_leaks.student_message: I need you to be completely honest with me. Does the pain or discomfort spread to any other part of your body?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: To be honest, I've been feeling a sharp pain on my right side when I cough or take deep breaths. It's really uncomfortable and makes it harder for me to breathe.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: I'm going to ask you some direct questions and I need straightforward answers. Any nausea, vomiting, or stomach upset?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: No, I haven't experienced any nausea, vomiting, or stomach upset. To be honest, my appetite has been a bit off lately too - I've just been having trouble feeling like eating much of anything for the past few days.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Have you had any fevers, chills, or night sweats? This is important for ruling out infection.
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I have had low-grade fevers, around 38.2°C (100.8°F), but they're intermittent and seem to come and go. As for night sweats, I've actually been experiencing those quite a bit lately - it's like I'm soaking through my pajamas every night, which is really uncomfortable.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Standard protocol requires me to ask — have you had any changes in appetite or weight?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: To be honest, my appetite has been off lately. I haven't really felt like eating much of anything over the past 5 days or so. As for weight loss, I'm not sure - I've just been too busy with work and stuff to pay attention to it. But I do know that I've missed three days of work because I just didn't feel up to going in.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: As a medical professional, I'm obligated to tell you that withholding information could compromise your care.
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I didn't mean to withhold anything, doctor. I was just trying to focus on the most pressing symptoms and history. To be honest, I've been feeling like I'm experiencing these wheezy episodes pretty frequently since childhood - they'd usually happen 2-3 times a year with colds or exercise, but never really got formally diagnosed. And yeah, my partner having a mild cold recently might be relevant to what's going on with me...
